steps.title: Connect zeros to the graph
steps.content: The equation $f(x)=0$ asks where the graph has a height of $0$. Those are exactly the points where the graph intersects the $x$-axis.

steps.title: Count the intersections
steps.content: On the shown graph, the curve never touches or crosses the $x$-axis. That means there are $0$ values of $x$ for which $f(x)=0$. The answer is D.
